questions.statement:
<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction:&nbsp;</span>Choose the word which is most nearly the&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: 700;">SAME&nbsp;</span>in the meaning as the word printed in&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: 700;">Bold</span>.</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Gamut</span></p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.</p>

<p>The office management was <u>in dire straits</u> after the financial crash.</p>
options: To walk on already trodden paths /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<div>Gear up for&nbsp;</div>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Directions:&nbsp;</span>Choose the word which is the exact OPPOSITE of the given words:</p>

<p>EXORBITANT&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Vent your spleen</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</p>

<p>Decadence&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Arraign</p>

<p><strong>Choose the word which best expresses the opposite meaning of the word.</strong></p>

<p>FOMENT</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Directions:</span>&nbsp;Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words/groups of words. Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.</p>

<p>Justice prevailed and the murderer was&nbsp;<u>decapitated</u>.</p>
